generated_oml:
// Sensing Components
instance LiDARSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-resolution 3D LiDAR sensor used to capture point cloud data at 10–20 Hz."
    DTDFVocab:producedData LidarPointCloudTransmitted
]

instance MotorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Engine dynamic sensor measuring motor RPM and torque."
    DTDFVocab:producedData MotorDataTransmitted
]

instance SteeringServoSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Steering servo sensor measuring servo angle."
    DTDFVocab:producedData SteeringServoDataTransmitted
]

instance VehicleOdometrySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Vehicle odometry sensor measuring speed, heading, and acceleration."
    DTDFVocab:producedData VehicleOdometryDataTransmitted
]

// Data Transmitted (ROS2 messages)
instance LidarPointCloudTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LiDARSensor
]

instance MotorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom MotorSensor
]

instance SteeringServoDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom SteeringServoSensor
]

instance VehicleOdometryDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom VehicleOdometrySensor
]

// Data (serialized ROS2 payload)
instance LidarPointCloudData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "LiDAR point cloud data streamed at 10–20 Hz."
    DTDFVocab:fromData LidarPointCloudTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance MotorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Motor RPM and torque data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData MotorDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance SteeringServoData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Steering servo angle data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData SteeringServoDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

instance VehicleOdometryData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Vehicle speed, heading, and acceleration data."
    DTDFVocab:fromData VehicleOdometryDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

// Enablers (infrastructure)
instance DataTranslationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Translates raw sensor data into standardized format, performing unit conversion and synchronization."
    DTDFVocab:enables DataMonitoringService,
                    ComputationOffloadingService,
                    RemoteManagementService
]

// Models (processing)
instance PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Processes raw LiDAR point cloud data to detect, track objects, and project driving trajectories."
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataTranslationEnabler
]

// Services
instance DataMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Allows operators to view, save, and generate reports of real-time vehicle state, driving path, and object tracks."
    DTDFVocab:provides ViewVehicleStateInsight,
                    ObjectTrackingInsight,
                    GenerateReportAction
]

instance ComputationOffloadingService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Offloads ACC algorithm to the DT, computes desired speed based on lead vehicle distance, and returns speed command to vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:provides ComputeDesiredSpeedAction,
                    ACCStatusInsight
]

instance RemoteManagementService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Enables operators to enable/disable ACC, set desired speed, and issue emergency braking commands with low latency."
    DTDFVocab:provides EnableACCAction,
                    SetDesiredSpeedAction,
                    EmergencyBrakeAction,
                    ACCStatusInsight
]

// Actions
instance GenerateReportAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Generates a report of vehicle state data."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance ComputeDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Computes desired speed for ACC based on detected distance to lead vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EnableACCAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Enables or disables adaptive cruise control."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance SetDesiredSpeedAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Sets a specified desired speed for the vehicle."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance EmergencyBrakeAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Triggers emergency braking command."
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

// Insights
instance ViewVehicleStateInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Provides real-time visualization of vehicle state."
]

instance ObjectTrackingInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Shows current tracked objects and projected trajectories."
]

instance ACCStatusInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Shows current ACC enablement status and desired speed."
]

// Environments (entities)
instance PhysicalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Physical twin entity containing sensors and automotive functions."
    base:contains LiDARSensor,
                     MotorSensor,
                     SteeringServoSensor,
                     VehicleOdometrySensor
]

instance DigitalTwinEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "Digital twin entity managing data processing, control logic, and state propagation."
    base:contains DataTranslationEnabler,
                     PointCloudClusteringTrackingModel
]

instance UserEntity : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "User interface entity providing visualization and remote control interfaces."
    base:contains DataMonitoringService,
                     ComputationOffloadingService,
                     RemoteManagementService
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "Control commands generated by the Digital Twin’s Control Panel User Interface (UI) are transmitted as ROS 2 messages through the Actuation FE to the physical actuators (motor, steering servo). Command types include throttle, brake, and steering angle, although the specific message schemas are not defined in the documentation. The Actuation FE validates incoming messages and forwards them to the corresponding physical device drivers. Feedback from the vehicle state sensors is continually streamed back through the Data Translation component, allowing the Digital Twin to close the loop. No explicit authentication or conflict‑resolution mechanisms are described."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "Devices sharing the same clock notion require strict synchronization; many safety‑critical vehicular standards mandate CO‑ordinated timekeeping tighter than 100 ms, but the present DTS is limited to 100 ms accuracy due to hardware constraints. All communication and data correlation between physical and digital entities rely on timestamps with ~100 ms granularity; computation‑offloading and event‑driven services operate on this temporal resolution."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The overall orchestration follows the ISO 23247 Reference Architecture, mapping each Software Entity (FE) to a ROS2 package that contains one or more ROS2 nodes. The SW components are first identified in a design phase, then mapped onto functional entities (Physical, Digital, User) and finally to physical deployment nodes. Each entity is instantiated on distinct hardware (e.g., on-board computer for Physical, edge server for Digital, display unit for User). The system leverages ROS2’s publish‑subscribe middleware for intra‑vehicle communication, with Topic naming and QoS profiles tuned to meet the 100 ms synchronization requirement. Data management and storage are handled by a dedicated Data‑Storage component that is not directly aligned to an ISO 23247 FE, reflecting a pragmatic deviation for engineering efficiency."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "System inter‑component communication occurs within a ROS 2 network, using DDS as the underlying protocol. Inter‑entity communication, including data pushed from on‑board physical entities to the Digital Twin, uses a Wi‑Fi network employing UDP at the transport layer. The Data Translation component serialises, aligns message types, and performs unit conversion for every data stream. Specific bandwidth or latency requirements are not specified. The architecture assumes a star topology centered on the vehicle’s onboard computing unit, with forwarders for Wi‑Fi to external telemetry. Security protocols such as TLS or VPN are not referenced in the available documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin architecture is installed in the vehicle’s onboard computing environment (edge deployment). ROS 2 nodes run locally, and sensor data and actuator commands are transmitted over the internal Wi‑Fi network (UDP). The system relies on the vehicle’s embedded processors and storage, but specific resource demands (CPU, memory, storage) are not quantified. No cloud or on‑premise external hosting is described."
]

instance data_ownership_and_privacy : DTDFVocab:DataOwnershipPrivacy [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin’s data governance follows the privacy and data‑protection framework defined in 3GPP TS 22.185 v15.0.0 Release 15. All V2X messages are signed with a vehicle‑specific X‑509 digital certificate issued by an ITS Service Provider Certificate Authority (CA) and encrypted with AES-128-GCM, providing integrity and confidentiality. Personal data such as GPS coordinates are anonymised using hash‑redaction before storage or sharing. User consent for data collection is captured via an over-the-air consent exchange and is recorded in an audit log. When the twin transmits data to third‑party services, it enforces consent‑based data‑sharing agreements that comply with the GDPR‑aligned privacy requirements of 3GPP TS 22.185. All internal data flows are protected by role-based access control and firewall‑segmented networks."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system adheres to ISO 23247 – Digital Twin Framework for Manufacturing, specifically referencing Part 2 (Reference Architecture). The architecture is entity‑based, comprising Observable Manufacturing Element (OME), Data Collection and Device Control Entity, Digital Twin Entity, and User Entity, each with Functional Entities (e.g., actuators, data transformers, user interfaces). While ISO 23247 does not prescribe implementation details, the system architecture may incorporate OPC Unified Architecture (OPC UA) for device communication and data exchange. The reference implementation and demonstration can be consulted at https://github.com/McSCert/Automotive-Digital-Twin and the accompanying demo at https://youtu.be/Rv-sJJQc3WU."
]

instance security_and_safety_considerations : DTDFVocab:SecuritySafety [
    base:desc "Safety-critical functions of the Digital Twin conform to ISO 26262-1:2018. Hazard analysis and risk assessment (HRA) assign ASIL levels to Adaptive Cruise Control (ACC) components. Redundant control paths, each monitored by cyclic diagnostics on every 10 ms hardware tick, trigger a roll‑back to a safe state when a fault or anomaly is detected, ensuring a fail-safe disengagement of ACC. Secure software delivery is achieved through a secure‑boot chain-of-trust and signed binaries. Cyber‑security protects the V2X interface per 3GPP TS 22.185: messages are digitally signed by X‑509 certificates, integrity protected by HMAC-SHA256, and confidentiality secured with AES-128-GCM. Threat mitigation includes an intrusion detection system (IDS) that flags anomalous message patterns, and a network segmentation policy isolates critical safety nodes. Run-time monitoring continuously checks for deviations from the safety concept and, if needed, isolated services are transitioned into a degraded but safe mode."
]
